text:
President Trump questioned whether Iran’s new supreme leader was alive, fueling speculation about the health of Mojtaba Khamenei, who was named his father’s successor a week ago, but hasn’t been seen in public in his new role.
“I’m hearing he’s not alive and, if he is, he should do something very smart for his country and that’s surrender,” Mr. Trump told NBC News in an interview late Saturday, without providing details or evidence to support his statement.
Mr. Trump’s comments added to rumors about the health of Ayatollah Khamenei. On Thursday, Iranian state media released a statement attributed to Ayatollah Khamenei that directed the military to continue blocking a vital oil shipping route and called for Iran’s neighbors to close U.S. military bases used to attack Iran. Iranian leaders have said they are keeping him out of the public eye because any communication could reveal his location and put him in danger.
title: Here is the latest.
